pattern: Every match position spreads a fixed-radius window of eligible indices around it, so the answer is the union of those windows clipped to the array bounds.
hints: First find every index where the value equals key; every k-distant index sits within distance k of at least one of those.
Instead of checking each index against every match, mark a window of length 2k+1 around each match and take the union.
Sort the matches (they are already in increasing order during a left-to-right scan), then sweep once collecting max(0, m-k) .. min(n-1, m+k) for each match m into a sorted, deduplicated result.

edge_inputs.description: two separated matches with overlapping windows
every index is within range of some match
single element array equal to key
k equal to array length, whole array becomes k-distant
match sits at index 0, windows must clip instead of going negative
match sits at the last index, window must clip at n-1
key never actually appears near some far-away index, which must be excluded

stress.description: 1000 elements with a sparse scattering of the key and small k, forcing genuine window-union logic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    key = rng.randrange(1, 1001)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    num_hits = max(1, n // 20)
    for _ in range(num_hits):
        nums[rng.randrange(n)] = key
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [nums, key, k]
stress.note: guarantees at least one occurrence of key so the answer is always well-defined
